name: standup
description: Local-first daily stand-up generator. Watches your git, IDE activity & GitHub, drafts your stand-up with a local LLM, posts to Discord/Slack.

standup.lookbackHours.description: How many hours back to gather activity when generating a stand-up.

standup.ollama.url.description: Base URL of your local Ollama server.

standup.ollama.model.description: Ollama model to use for summarizing your activity.

standup.github.useBuiltInAuth.markdownDescription: Use the editor's built-in GitHub sign-in instead of a manual token. No PAT needed — run 'Standup: Connect GitHub' once. Ignored if `standup.github.token` is set.

standup.github.token.markdownDescription: Optional GitHub personal access token. Only needed if you prefer NOT to use built-in sign-in (e.g. headless use). Leave empty to use built-in auth.

standup.github.repos.markdownDescription: Repos to check, as `owner/name`. If empty, Standup auto-detects from the current workspace's git `origin` remote.

enumDescriptions: 5-7 points with the key specifics (files, endpoints, issue numbers)
5-8 thorough points enumerating files, assets, fields and URLs — a full work log
standup.detail.description: How detailed the generated stand-up should be. Also switchable from the stand-up panel.

standup.webhook.type.description: Where the Send button posts your stand-up.

standup.webhook.url.markdownDescription: Incoming webhook URL for Discord or Slack. No bot or OAuth needed — create an incoming webhook in your channel settings.

standup.track.enabled.description: Track IDE activity (which files you edit and save). Stored locally only.

standup.reminder.enabled.description: Show a daily local-time notification reminding you to send your stand-up.

standup.reminder.time.markdownDescription: Daily reminder time in 24h `HH:MM`, local time. Set on first run; change any time here or via 'Standup: Set Reminder Time'.

standup.cursor.enabled.markdownDescription: Include the AI prompts you gave Cursor's assistant today as context for the stand-up. Read locally & read-only from Cursor's chat DB; nothing is uploaded. No effect if Cursor isn't installed.
